Home › About

About the program

Over ten weeks, fellows move from onboarding to shipped code — working in small teams on real problems the department’s own WordPress infrastructure faces.

By the numbers

Program

10

Weeks, Paid

A full summer arc — from paid onboarding in week one to the department showcase in week ten. Fellows are paid for every week of the program, including onboarding.

Research

4

Research Tracks

Plugin architecture, editor and block UX, performance and security, and accessibility — each track ships real code into the department’s own WordPress infrastructure.

Mentorship

3–4

Fellows per Team

Small teams led by a faculty mentor and a graduate TA, with weekly one-on-ones, code review, and a Friday all-hands demo keeping every fellow accountable.

Research tracks

Plugin Architecture

Design and ship extensible plugins for the department’s WordPress infrastructure — custom post types, settings APIs, and REST endpoints built to production standards. Learn more.

Editor & Block UX

Build Gutenberg blocks and editor experiences that content teams actually enjoy using, pairing React and the block APIs with real editorial workflows. Learn more.

Performance & Security

Profile, harden, and speed up live sites — from query optimization and caching to input validation, capability checks, and dependency audits. Learn more.

Accessibility

Make interfaces work for everyone, testing against WCAG with assistive technology and folding accessibility into the build rather than bolting it on. Learn more.

How the summer runs

  1. Week 1

    Paid onboarding

    Local environment setup, a tour of WordPress internals, and team formation across the four tracks.

  2. Weeks 2–4

    Scoping

    Problem scoping with your faculty mentor, ending in a first working prototype you can demo.

  3. Weeks 5–8

    Core build

    The main build sprint, with weekly code review and a Friday all-hands demo to keep momentum honest.

  4. Week 9

    Polish

    Testing, documentation, and the final round of review before the work goes in front of the department.

  5. Week 10

    Showcase

    The end-of-summer showcase, presenting shipped work to faculty and department staff.

Faculty mentorship

Weekly 1:1s and code review

Every fellow joins a group of three to four students led by a faculty mentor and a graduate TA. Weekly one-on-ones, code review, and a Friday all-hands demo keep fellows accountable — mentors also help scope a capstone-ready portfolio piece from the summer’s work. Learn more.
